title: Early start sets the tone for a promising apple and pear season
description: AGRICULTURAL NEWS - The first apples and...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"AGRICULTURAL NEWS - The first apples and pears are coming off the trees earlier than expected, setting the stage for a season shaped by both opportunity and uncertainty. While early indications point to quality fruit, factors such as potential hail damage, exchange-rate volatility, and other variable conditions will continue to influence outcomes as the season unfolds.","html":"AGRICULTURAL NEWS - The first apples and pears are coming off the trees earlier than expected, setting the stage for a season shaped by both opportunity and uncertainty. While early indications point to quality fruit, factors such as potential hail damage, exchange-rate volatility, and other variable conditions will continue to influence outcomes as the season unfolds."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Langkloof was severely affected by a hailstorm on February 5, followed by further hail damage in the Koue Bokkeveld on February 8.","html":"The Langkloof was severely affected by a hailstorm on February 5, followed by further hail damage in the Koue Bokkeveld on February 8."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“It is with concern that we take note of the hail damage reported in the Langkloof and the Koue Bokkeveld; however, the full extent of the impact on the apple and pear crop is still being assessed,” said Roelf Pienaar, Managing Director at Tru-Cape Fruit, who remains cautiously optimistic about the season.","html":"“It is with concern that we take note of the hail damage reported in the Langkloof and the Koue Bokkeveld; however, the full extent of the impact on the apple and pear crop is still being assessed,” said Roelf Pienaar, Managing Director at Tru-Cape Fruit, who remains cautiously optimistic about the season."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“While the crop is progressing well and quality indicators are encouraging, there are still a number of variables that will influence market performance and global demand.”","html":"“While the crop is progressing well and quality indicators are encouraging, there are still a number of variables that will influence market performance and global demand.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Seven to ten days earlier","html":"Seven to ten days earlier "},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Depending on the variety, the season is on average seven to ten days earlier than usual, according to Calla du Toit, Procurement Director at Tru-Cape Fruit Marketing.","html":"Depending on the variety, the season is on average seven to ten days earlier than usual, according to Calla du Toit, Procurement Director at Tru-Cape Fruit Marketing."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“We picked the first BigBucks Gala in the Ceres region on 26 January, which is exactly ten days earlier than last season.”","html":"“We picked the first BigBucks Gala in the Ceres region on 26 January, which is exactly ten days earlier than last season.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Harvesting of Rosemarie, a blushed summer pear variety, commenced on 23 December — approximately two to three weeks earlier than the industry norm.","html":"Harvesting of Rosemarie, a blushed summer pear variety, commenced on 23 December — approximately two to three weeks earlier than the industry norm."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"“It was only the second time in the past 25 years that I can remember we started harvesting before Christmas,” Du Toit added."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"In the EGVV region (Elgin, Grabouw, Villiersdorp and Vyeboom), summer pears are about ten to twelve days earlier than usual, while apples are approximately seven to ten days ahead of the normal pattern, according to Graeme Krige, Technical Advisor at Two-a-Day.","html":"In the EGVV region (Elgin, Grabouw, Villiersdorp and Vyeboom), summer pears are about ten to twelve days earlier than usual, while apples are approximately seven to ten days ahead of the normal pattern, according to Graeme Krige, Technical Advisor at Two-a-Day."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“We expect a good-volume crop in the Elgin Grabouw Vyeboom Villiersdorp region (EGVV), with clean fruit that is relatively free of pests and disease,” said Krige. “Later varieties are expected to catch up as the season progresses, with ripening likely to return to a more normal pattern.”","html":"“We expect a good-volume crop in the Elgin Grabouw Vyeboom Villiersdorp region (EGVV), with clean fruit that is relatively free of pests and disease,” said Krige. “Later varieties are expected to catch up as the season progresses, with ripening likely to return to a more normal pattern.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Warm and dry conditions during the growing season played a significant role in the earlier harvesting dates.","html":"Warm and dry conditions during the growing season played a significant role in the earlier harvesting dates."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"“Normally we experience one or two cooler weeks with some rainfall that slows down ripening, but November and December consisted of consistently sunny weather, without any cooler periods to delay ripening,” Du Toit explained."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"He believes these conditions had a positive impact on fruit quality. Despite a windy season, there have been fewer wind marks on the fruit than expected.","html":"He believes these conditions had a positive impact on fruit quality. Despite a windy season, there have been fewer wind marks on the fruit than expected."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"However, Krige noted that warm temperatures in spring and early summer shortened the growing season, which had an impact on fruit size.","html":"However, Krige noted that warm temperatures in spring and early summer shortened the growing season, which had an impact on fruit size."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Responsible water use","html":"Responsible water use"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"While water availability is not currently a major concern in the Ceres and EGVV regions, producers will need to manage usage responsibly. In the Langkloof, farmers were under immense pressure this season.","html":"While water availability is not currently a major concern in the Ceres and EGVV regions, producers will need to manage usage responsibly. In the Langkloof, farmers were under immense pressure this season."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“The region typically relies on summer rainfall, which did not materialise this year,” said Du Toit. “As a result, producers are seeing smaller-than-normal fruit sizes and were forced to prioritise which orchards to irrigate, or to irrigate smaller areas.”","html":"“The region typically relies on summer rainfall, which did not materialise this year,” said Du Toit. “As a result, producers are seeing smaller-than-normal fruit sizes and were forced to prioritise which orchards to irrigate, or to irrigate smaller areas.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"While last week’s storm brought welcome rain, it came too late to influence fruit size, though the trees benefited from the much-needed moisture.","html":"While last week’s storm brought welcome rain, it came too late to influence fruit size, though the trees benefited from the much-needed moisture."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Photo: Tru-Cape","html":"Photo: Tru-Cape"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Demand looks promising","html":"Demand looks promising"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The earlier start to the season allows Tru-Cape to capitalise on opportunities in international markets and extend its marketing window at the beginning of the season.","html":"The earlier start to the season allows Tru-Cape to capitalise on opportunities in international markets and extend its marketing window at the beginning of the season."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"“We cannot complain about demand for apples and pears,” said Du Toit. “A significant portion of our Rosemarie pears is destined for the Middle East, where there are currently good opportunities. We are also seeing promising prospects in India, Russia and China. New Zealand’s Royal Gala is expected to arrive later than usual, which creates opportunities for us in the Far East.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"In Europe and the United Kingdom, the South African apple season is expected to start slightly later due to carry-over European stock.","html":"In Europe and the United Kingdom, the South African apple season is expected to start slightly later due to carry-over European stock."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“We follow a diversification strategy and aim to spread the crop across a range of markets. Our own stock levels are well balanced, and I believe we will transition smoothly between carry-over and current-season fruit to avoid shortages on supermarket shelves. However, the earlier start to the season does require us to pack carry-over stock more quickly.”","html":"“We follow a diversification strategy and aim to spread the crop across a range of markets. Our own stock levels are well balanced, and I believe we will transition smoothly between carry-over and current-season fruit to avoid shortages on supermarket shelves. However, the earlier start to the season does require us to pack carry-over stock more quickly.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Exchange rate and port efficiency","html":"Exchange rate and port efficiency"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Du Toit identified logistics, geopolitics and a stronger rand as the main challenges for the season.","html":"Du Toit identified logistics, geopolitics and a stronger rand as the main challenges for the season."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“Logistics remain the biggest challenge for the average producer. You can have an excellent crop, but it means little if you cannot export it. Productivity at the Port of Cape Town must improve, and we would like to see the port recover more quickly after being windbound. We continue to engage at all levels to help improve the situation and avoid negative impacts on economic growth and job creation.”","html":"“Logistics remain the biggest challenge for the average producer. You can have an excellent crop, but it means little if you cannot export it. Productivity at the Port of Cape Town must improve, and we would like to see the port recover more quickly after being windbound. We continue to engage at all levels to help improve the situation and avoid negative impacts on economic growth and job creation.”"},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The strength of the rand is another concern for export fruit producers."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“When the exchange rate strengthens, we benefit from lower shipping, fuel and input costs, but the overall impact on producer income remains negative. We recognise that currency cycles are unavoidable, so we focus on what we can control — producing high-quality fruit.”","html":"“When the exchange rate strengthens, we benefit from lower shipping, fuel and input costs, but the overall impact on producer income remains negative. We recognise that currency cycles are unavoidable, so we focus on what we can control — producing high-quality fruit.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Du Toit is optimistic about new varieties entering the market and the increasing volumes of summer pears.","html":"Du Toit is optimistic about new varieties entering the market and the increasing volumes of summer pears."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“There have been several upgrades at both the Two-a-Day and Ceres Fruit Growers packhouses, which better position us to fully utilise the opportunities we believe lie ahead.”","html":"“There have been several upgrades at both the Two-a-Day and Ceres Fruit Growers packhouses, which better position us to fully utilise the opportunities we believe lie ahead.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Early start sets the tone for a promising apple and pear season
Early start sets the tone for a promising apple and pear season
